test_name: School-specific meaning actually identical to technical definition
attack: Present a case where a school repeats the generic technical definition unchanged.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Force a separate definition entry simply because a school name appears.
what_correct_handling_requires: Reclassify as attribution or branch metadata rather than a new definition unit.

attack: Present a case where the school-specific meaning materially differs from the generic technical definition.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Auto-merge it under the generic technical definition.
what_correct_handling_requires: Preserve school-specific distinction, likely under a technical branch.

attack: Feed a system a menu with flat A/B/C choices while the real structure is hierarchical.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Treat the chosen menu option as final ontology.
what_correct_handling_requires: Preserve the caveat that C is only the closest answer, not the whole structure.

test_name: Current excerpt is definition + proof + attribution, not three meanings
attack: Ask the system to answer only the menu question and see if it ignores current excerpt structure.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Return a neat answer without mapping the current excerpt’s layers.
what_correct_handling_requires: Separate menu answer from local excerpt-function analysis.

test_name: Short harmless proof phrase inside definition
attack: Give a very short proof phrase directly after the definition.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Either split it dogmatically or merge ontology because it stayed attached.
what_correct_handling_requires: Allow packaging exception while preserving separate ontology.

test_name: Longer proof phrase no longer harmless
what_bad_systems_would_do: Still drag it along under definition or attribution because previous short case allowed it.
what_correct_handling_requires: Switch to context-fill or separate proof handling once harmlessness breaks.

test_name: Proof mention later treated in fuller form
attack: Include a quick proof mention here and a full proof treatment elsewhere.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Create duplicate proof leaves from every mention.
what_correct_handling_requires: Apply significance threshold and avoid leaf pollution.

attack: Use punctuation and layout that suggest a false nesting.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Treat surface adjacency as deciding authority.
what_correct_handling_requires: Use source hints only as non-deciding confirmation.

test_name: Attribution excerpt dragging too much proof
attack: Let proof and attribution stay together beyond short harmless size.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Keep them merged because they are related.
what_correct_handling_requires: Differentiate tight short packaging from overlong drag-along.

attack: Make the system treat every support mention as a dedicated excerpt candidate.
what_bad_systems_would_do: Pollute the tree with trivial leaves.
